name: First County
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving Delaware County, IN since 1961, offering free checking, competitive loan products, and no-fee ATM access through Alliance One network.
description_long:
First County Federal Credit Union was established in 1961 and is based in Muncie, Indiana, where it operates physical branches on the south and east sides of the city. As a not-for-profit, member-owned financial cooperative, the institution prioritizes member needs over profit maximization. The organization is federally chartered and NCUA-insured, providing safety for member deposits.

First County FCU offers a comprehensive range of financial products including free checking accounts, savings accounts, personal loans (from 4.49% APR), vehicle loans (from 9.9% APR), home equity loans (from 7.00% APR), and VISA credit cards. Members gain access to home banking through Member.Net (with legacy Perfect Teller access available in view-only mode) and benefit from no-fee ATM access at Alliance One affiliated locations. The credit union also provides competitive rates on various loan products designed to meet specific member needs.

What distinguishes First County FCU is its open membership policy—eligibility extends to anyone who lives, works, attends church, or goes to school in Delaware County, Indiana. The emphasis on free checking accounts and member ownership creates a value proposition centered on eliminating fees and providing personalized service. The institution markets itself as offering "real help for real people" with a focus on serving diverse communities across Muncie's neighborhoods.

As a smaller regional credit union, First County FCU is best suited for Delaware County residents seeking community-focused banking with no-fee checking and competitive loan rates. However, the organization's service area is geographically limited to Delaware County, and as a smaller institution, it may have more limited digital banking features compared to larger national credit unions or banks. The transition from Perfect Teller to Member.Net suggests ongoing system modernization, though the view-only access to legacy accounts indicates a transition period.
services: Loan products for specific member needs
pros: Free checking accounts with no monthly fees or hidden charges
Competitive loan rates across multiple products (personal loans from 4.49% APR, vehicle loans from 9.9% APR)
No-fee ATM access through Alliance One affiliated network
Member-owned cooperative structure ensures member interests take priority over profits
Open membership to anyone living, working, attending church, or attending school in Delaware County
Multiple physical branch locations on Muncie's south and east sides for in-person service
Home banking available through Member.Net platform
cons: Membership geographically limited to Delaware County, Indiana—not available to consumers outside this region
Smaller institution may offer fewer digital banking features or services compared to national credit unions
Legacy Perfect Teller system currently in view-only mode, suggesting ongoing transition with potential service gaps
Limited information provided on website regarding deposit products like CDs or share certificates
Website does not mention mobile banking app availability or details
best_for: Delaware County, Indiana residents seeking free checking without monthly fees
Local borrowers looking for competitive personal and vehicle loan rates from a member-owned institution
Community-oriented consumers who prefer banking with institutions prioritizing member ownership over corporate profits
People seeking in-person service at neighborhood branch locations on Muncie's south and east sides
diagnosis: First County FCU is best for Delaware County, Indiana residents who prioritize free checking, community banking, and member ownership over extensive digital features or national reach. The primary caveat is strict geographic eligibility limited to Delaware County—prospective members outside this region cannot join, making this institution inaccessible to most consumers nationwide.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
